dataSource: Costco historical records, SEC filings, news reports

timeline.event: Costco opens first warehouse in Seattle; hot dog and soda combo introduced at $1.50
Expansion to California and beyond; combo remains $1.50
Costco becomes top warehouse club; food court popular with members
Dot-com boom; Costco hot dog gains cult following online
Switch from Coca-Cola to Pepsi partnership; combo still $1.50
Costco switches from Hebrew National to Kirkland Signature hot dogs
Craig Jelinek tells Jim Sinegal he may need to raise price; Sinegal says 'If you raise the price, I will kill you'
Costco sells over 100 million hot dogs annually; $1.50 price becomes iconic
COVID-19 pandemic; food court service disrupted but price holds
Inflation surges; analysts speculate about price increase; Costco reaffirms commitment
Costco CFO confirms 'The price is safe' amid record inflation
Over 150 million hot dogs sold; $1.50 remains a loss leader for membership retention
New CEO Ron Vachris takes over; $1.50 commitment continues
No price change; Costco explores food court modernization while maintaining classic pricing
41 years at $1.50 — longest-running fast-food price in American history

rumors.claim: Costco may raise hot dog price to $2.00 due to inflation
Soda cup size reduction signals impending price hike
rumors.resolution: Cup size reduced from 20oz to 16oz; price still $1.50

rumors.claim: Costco testing $2.50 combo in select markets

inflationComparison.title: Costco Hot Dog vs Other Prices: 1985–2026
inflationComparison.note: If the $1.50 hot dog had tracked general inflation, it would cost $4.35+ today. Meanwhile, gas, movie tickets, and Big Macs all rose dramatically.
